text: The grammar of the Hebrew text is obscure; it runs, literally, ‘It turns about as clay (of?) the signet-ring; they stand like a garment’.
The constellations mentioned cannot be identified with certainty.
The two words represented here by ‘heart’ and ‘the cock’ are variously interpreted by Hebrew scholars.
In the Hebrew text, ‘Who is wise enough to count the stars, or can lay down the water-skins (or perhaps, the lutes) of heaven?’
